entries.title: Live Journal Auto Entry — 2026-02-21 09:45 AM PST
Trading Journal — Monday, February 23, 2026
entries.content: Session summary: 3 trades logged. Gross PnL -119.50, commissions 3.00, net PnL -122.50. Win/Loss/BE = 1/2/0 (Winrate 33.3%). Best trade: MAN-20260223-03 (MNQH6) 7.50. Worst trade: MAN-20260223-02 (MNQH6) -69.00.
entries.setupContext: Volatile session with bearish pressure early, then recovery attempts around key intraday levels. Used 2x BOS + FVG retest logic and A+ window filters.
entries.execution: Trade 1 lacked platform stop and overshot planned risk. Trade 2 used restored ATM and honored invalidation. Trade 3 long used 250/125 plan, moved stop to BE, and exited small green.
entries.psychology: Initial frustration after avoidable loss, then regained control by slowing down and following TP-or-BE rules.
entries.ruleAdherence: Improved through session: stop discipline and no revenge entries after losses.
entries.whatIDidGood: Recovered discipline mid-session, restored ATM stop workflow, and executed TP-or-BE management without revenge trading.
entries.whatToImprove: Set stop order before every entry, avoid manual stop tracking, and reduce over-management during stall candles.
entries.lessonLearned: No stop order in market = no trade. Execution tools must be set before entry.
entries.nextTradeRule: Before any entry: confirm ATM template loaded, SL/TP visible, and size valid.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Tuesday, February 24, 2026
entries.mood: Calm finish; process-first despite choppy tape
entries.content: Session summary: 2 trades logged. Gross PnL -119.00, commissions 2.00, net PnL -121.00. Win/Loss/BE = 0/2/0 (Winrate 0.0%). Best trade: MAN-20260224-00 (MNQH6) -55.00. Worst trade: MAN-20260224-01 (MNQH6) -64.00.
entries.setupContext: Choppy session with mixed lower-timeframe swings, but valid short thesis appeared with 2x BOS + FVG retest.
entries.execution: Entered MNQ short at 25000 with predefined SL 25032 and TP 24938.25. Price chopped and eventually tagged stop. No SL widening, no panic exit, no revenge trade.
entries.psychology: Some tension during OB retest, but stayed mechanical and followed invalidation rules.
entries.ruleAdherence: Strong: respected original stop and ended session after stop-out.
entries.whatIDidGood: Clear thesis-based entry, disciplined risk management, and emotional control after loss.
entries.whatToImprove: Avoid second-guessing during chop; trust pre-trade invalidation without micro-managing candles.
entries.lessonLearned: A disciplined stop-out is a good trade process-wise, even when outcome is red.
entries.nextTradeRule: If invalidation does not trigger, hold; if it triggers, exit clean and end session.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Wednesday, February 25, 2026
entries.mood: Disciplined under chop and event-risk pressure
entries.content: Session summary: 2 trades logged. Gross PnL -81.50, commissions 2.00, net PnL -83.50. Win/Loss/BE = 0/2/0 (Winrate 0.0%). Best trade: MAN-20260225-02 (MNQH6) -13.50. Worst trade: MAN-20260225-01 (MNQH6) -68.00.
entries.setupContext: Mixed/choppy session with multiple fake breaks and late-day NVDA earnings risk affecting conviction near highs.
entries.execution: Trade 1 long (25312.75) stopped at 25278.75 after setup failed. Trade 2 long (25346.25) was valid but extended; exited early at 25339.50 to protect capital before earnings-driven uncertainty.
entries.psychology: Managed FOMO better by respecting dead-zone rules and prioritizing process over chasing. Caught myself when hope-based thinking appeared.
entries.ruleAdherence: Good overall: respected stop, avoided dead-zone override, and made defensive exit decision on extended late-day conditions.
entries.whatIDidGood: Followed A+ checklist more consistently, identified hope-vs-rule distinction in real time, and protected capital during uncertain conditions.
entries.whatToImprove: Don’t anchor on one signal (e.g., ORB retest) when BOS structure conflicts. Keep execution checklist explicit before entry.
entries.lessonLearned: “I’ll exit near BE unless reclaim confirms” is rule-based; “maybe it comes back” is hope-based. BOS line/level is context, BOS event is break-confirm action.
entries.nextTradeRule: At +50% to TP, move SL to BE (or BE +1 tick) unless structure invalidation says otherwise.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Thursday, February 26, 2026
entries.content: Session summary: 4 trades logged. Gross PnL 21.50, commissions 4.00, net PnL 17.50. Win/Loss/BE = 3/1/0 (Winrate 75.0%). Best trade: MAN-20260226-04 (MNQH6) 44.00. Worst trade: MAN-20260226-02 (MNQH6) -62.50.
entries.setupContext: 4 executed trades. Focused on A+ setups, break-retest confirmations, and momentum context while enforcing max-trade discipline.
entries.execution: Trade 1: short +$13 (partial then protected). Trade 2: long stop-out -$62.50. Trade 3: long +$27 with context-managed deadzone exit. Trade 4: long +$44 momentum shift capture.
entries.psychology: Good emotional control after early frustration. Chose to stop despite missed later move and explicitly prioritized long-term discipline over overtrading.
entries.ruleAdherence: Improved: respected invalidations, took context-aware profits, and shut down after exceeding ideal trade count to avoid further impulse entries.
entries.whatIDidGood: Recovered quickly after loss, avoided revenge behavior, executed cleaner entries, and protected gains in lower-quality time windows.
entries.whatToImprove: Predefine whether trade is standard 2R hold or context-managed scalp before entry to reduce mid-trade negotiation.
entries.lessonLearned: Missing a move is acceptable; violating trade-count and quality rules is not. Process consistency compounds faster than forcing extra trades.
entries.nextTradeRule: Max 3 trades/day. If an exceptional 4th trade happens, it must be A+ only and then immediate shutdown.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Friday, February 27, 2026
entries.content: Session summary: 6 trades logged. Gross PnL 51.50, commissions 6.00, net PnL 45.50. Win/Loss/BE = 2/4/0 (Winrate 33.3%). Best trade: MAN-20260227-CC-01 (AAPL 285C 2026-03-20) 228.00. Worst trade: MAN-20260227-01 (MNQH6) -62.50.
entries.setupContext: Negative-gamma/choppy conditions with repeated fake breaks around ORB and intraday levels.
entries.execution: 5 MNQ trades from platform fills: 2 full stop-outs early, 1 small scalp winner, then 2 defensive exits. Added 1 covered-call overlay (AAPL 285C) for income; position remains open.
entries.psychology: Strong self-regulation improvements: repeatedly waited extra candles, avoided forced deadzone entries, and honored shutdown decision.
entries.ruleAdherence: Improving on discipline and defensive exits; entry timing still needs work in choppy context.
entries.whatIDidGood: Defensive exits, no revenge spiral, conscious pass decisions in chop, and willingness to stop forcing low-quality setups.
entries.whatToImprove: Wait for stronger confirmation and avoid early entries in chop; maintain no-long-below-10m-8EMA unless reclaim+hold.
entries.lessonLearned: In chop, patience and confirmation candles matter more than catching every move.
entries.nextTradeRule: No long below 10m 8EMA unless reclaim + hold. In uncertainty, wait one more candle (then one more if still unclear).

entries.title: Trading Journal — Monday, March 2, 2026
entries.mood: Disciplined under stress; one controlled loss with process awareness.
entries.content: Session summary: 1 trades logged. Gross PnL -62.50, commissions 1.00, net PnL -63.50. Win/Loss/BE = 0/1/0 (Winrate 0.0%). Best trade: 2026-03-02-mnqh6-0824-long-1 (MNQH6) -62.50. Worst trade: 2026-03-02-mnqh6-0824-long-1 (MNQH6) -62.50.
entries.setupContext: Mixed tape with selective opportunities. Futures setup quality was uneven, while options/covered-call management required tighter process focus.
entries.execution: Executed two logged trades for the day. MNQ long resulted in a controlled stop/loss. AAPL covered-call management was completed with defined entry/exit, preserving process structure rather than improvising mid-trade.
entries.psychology: Stayed composed after loss and avoided emotional escalation. Primary challenge was balancing speed with precision in execution decisions.
entries.ruleAdherence: Good overall. Hard risk controls were respected and no stop widening behavior was observed.
entries.whatIDidGood: Losses were contained, execution stayed structured, and emotional control remained intact after adverse movement.
entries.whatToImprove: Improve patience on futures confirmation and avoid early commitment when setup quality is borderline.
entries.lessonLearned: A controlled red on one trade is acceptable when process stays clean; consistency beats forcing recovery trades.
entries.nextTradeRule: Only take futures entries after full confirmation stack (close + reclaim/hold + clear invalidation). If setup is borderline, pass.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Tuesday, March 3, 2026
entries.mood: Honest self-assessment. Slight emotional tilt carried over from prior AAPL incident.
entries.content: Session summary: 10 trades logged. Gross PnL 134.50, commissions 10.00, net PnL 124.50. Win/Loss/BE = 8/2/0 (Winrate 80.0%). Best trade: MAN-20260303-02 (MNQH6) 44.00. Worst trade: MAN-20260303-09 (MNQH6) -62.50.
entries.setupContext: Market conditions were mostly choppy/dead-zone. Setups appeared tradable on checklist surface, but follow-through quality was poor and required stricter filtering.
entries.execution: Overtraded scalp-style in low-quality conditions. Main issue was not setup identification but process drift in execution and trade selection. Exit behavior leaned too protective too early due to prior emotional carryover.
entries.psychology: Underlying urgency to “do something” influenced decisions. Recognized that acceptable PnL can hide poor process quality, which is dangerous reinforcement.
entries.ruleAdherence: Partial. Checklist awareness existed, but dead-zone participation and overtrading behavior violated process intent.
entries.whatIDidGood: Honest review instead of rationalizing results. Identified root cause (process drift > strategy failure). Locked concrete reset rules for next session.
entries.whatToImprove: No CHOPPY/DEAD-ZONE trades without break + retest + hold. Improve exit structure discipline (no premature protection from fear). Reduce emotional carryover from prior-day mistakes.
entries.lessonLearned: Good outcomes with bad process are a trap. Process quality must be graded independently from PnL.
entries.nextTradeRule: No trade in chop/dead-zone unless clean break + retest + hold. Max 3 trades/day. Auto shutdown after second rule break.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Wednesday, March 4, 2026
entries.mood: More stable and process-aware. Intentional focus on consistency over outcome.
entries.content: Session summary: 1 trades logged. Gross PnL -66.50, commissions 0.00, net PnL -66.50. Win/Loss/BE = 0/1/0 (Winrate 0.0%). Best trade: manual-2026-03-04-bcd1fc75 (MNQ) -66.50. Worst trade: manual-2026-03-04-bcd1fc75 (MNQ) -66.50.
entries.setupContext: Mixed-quality long opportunities in noisy conditions. Edge existed only when reclaim confirmation was clear; support-hold-only entries were unreliable.
entries.execution: Multiple long attempts with mixed quality. One non-system catch-knife entry created avoidable drawdown. Best execution came from waiting for reclaim confirmation (ex: reclaim above 25204 with confirming closes) and honoring predefined SL/TP.
entries.psychology: Psychology improved after recognizing process leaks. No stop widening, no revenge escalation after losses. Stronger acceptance of stop-outs.
entries.ruleAdherence: Moderate-to-good. Broke process on one non-system entry, but recovered discipline and maintained hard risk controls.
entries.whatIDidGood: Honored stops. Avoided revenge spirals. Returned to confirmation-based entries. Kept consistency as priority.
entries.whatToImprove: Remove support-hold-only entries without full trigger sequence. Treat dead-zone as default no-trade unless rule-based exception triggers. Keep A+ filter strict when market quality drops.
entries.lessonLearned: No reclaim + no full setup = no trade. Capital preservation and process protection are valid wins in unclear tape.
entries.nextTradeRule: A+ setups only. Max 3 trades. Start 0.5x in noisy conditions. Dead-zone = no trade unless explicit confirmation rules are met.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Thursday, March 5, 2026
entries.mood: Emotionally tested early after two quick losses, then stabilized through honest self-awareness and process resets. Ended calmer and more selective.
entries.content: Session summary: 11 trades logged. Gross PnL 20.00, commissions 11.00, net PnL 9.00. Win/Loss/BE = 8/3/0 (Winrate 72.7%). Best trade: CSV-0086 (MNQH6) 52.50. Worst trade: CSV-0079 (MNQH6) -63.50.
entries.setupContext: Open was choppy with repeated fake reclaims and knife-catch temptation around 25125/25055. Later session offered quick scalp opportunities but still required strict confirmation due to neutral/disagreeing LTF context.
entries.execution: Started with two stopped longs (non-ideal sequence). Avoided several low-quality knife entries, used cooldown reset (stepped away to clean house), then scalped selectively to recover. One accidental limit-buy was exited quickly with control. Gross P&L finished green but near flat after costs.
entries.psychology: Main challenge was FOMO from missed earlier longs and urge to make back missed opportunities. Biggest win was identifying revenge/FOMO in real time, stepping away, and returning with structure-based decisions.
entries.ruleAdherence: Mixed to improving. Early rule leakage (revenge/FOMO influence), then strong adherence: hard stops honored, no stop widening, and better confirmation discipline (2x BOS + HL logic).
entries.whatIDidGood: 1) Honest self-diagnosis during session. 2) Took stops without widening. 3) Successful emotional reset and recovered control. 4) Stayed engaged with structured entry framework instead of random clicks.
entries.whatToImprove: 1) Enforce mandatory 15-min lockout immediately after first stop. 2) Do not let missed entries create emotional debt. 3) Skip neutral/no-zone conditions unless explicit override rules are met.
entries.lessonLearned: Today reinforced that process recovery is the true win. A near-flat day after early emotional pressure is a success when discipline is restored and preserved.
entries.nextTradeRule: No close + no retest hold = no trade. After any stop: 15-minute lockout. Missed move is not a setup. Structure decides stop; risk decides size.

entries.summary: overtraded because of frustration with Maverick

Mood: Emotionally tested; focus on discipline reset over outcome.
Recovery Metrics: Sleep N/A | Readiness N/A

Execution: Best trade 49.0, worst trade -64.5. Prioritize A+ confirmation entries only.
Psychology: Pattern risk: protect against FOMO entries and red-to-green panic exits.

What I Did Good: Kept data logged and reviewed process objectively.
What To Improve: Avoid early entries before full confirmation; do not flatten just because trade turns green.

Session summary: Auto EOD journal generated at 1 PM PT and saved to MVP.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Tuesday, March 10, 2026
entries.mood: Clear self-awareness after execution drift; finished with shutdown discipline and reset mindset.
entries.content: Session summary: 33 trades logged. Gross PnL -107.00, commissions 33.00, net PnL -140.00. Win/Loss/BE = 21/11/1 (Winrate 63.6%). Best trade: CSV-20260310-432293461047-432293461019 (MNQH6) 89.50 net. Worst trade: CSV-20260310-400171097090-400171097098 (MNQH6) -130.00 net.
entries.setupContext: Two account cycles were active (v1 reset flow + v2 prop flow). Early session showed impulse entries and rapid re-entries before setups fully confirmed.
entries.execution: Main leak was premature entries and giving losers too much room while protecting winners too early. End-of-day behavior improved: explicit shutdown decision and process-first reset.
entries.psychology: Key realization: fear of giving back profit drove premature exits; urgency/FOMO drove early entries. Strong positive: named the pattern in real time and committed to rule-based correction.
entries.ruleAdherence: Mixed. Risk controls were present but setup-completion discipline was inconsistent early; adherence improved after defining hard rules and stopping for the day.
entries.whatIDidGood: 1) Honest diagnosis of execution leak. 2) Logged and reconciled both v1/v2 trades into MVP. 3) Shut down instead of forcing late recovery. 4) Committed to explicit anti-impulse SOP.
entries.whatToImprove: 1) No entry before 100% setup completion. 2) Mandatory 3-minute wait when urgency appears. 3) Mandatory 3-minute reset after stop-out before any re-entry. 4) Hold winners to plan unless invalidated.
entries.lessonLearned: I trade setups, not candles. Protecting process beats protecting floating PnL.
entries.nextTradeRule: No 100% setup = no trade. If urgency appears, wait 3 minutes. After stop-out on same idea, 3-minute reset before re-entry review. Max 3 trades/day.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Wednesday, March 11, 2026
entries.mood: Started focused but emotionally reactive around entries/exits. Mid-session got calmer and more process-driven. Ended with better control and less urgency/FOMO.
entries.content: Session summary: Red day with clear process lessons. Net P&L after $1/trade commissions was $-131.00 on 21 trades. Win rate was 66.7% (14W / 7L). Biggest issue remained loss size versus average win, even though execution improved later in the session.
entries.setupContext: Primary instrument: MNQ. Traded mostly long setups around reclaim/retest structure. Conditions were often choppy around key zones with multiple fake reclaim attempts. Several FL0WG0D flow signals were reviewed, but execution still depended on chart confirmation.
entries.execution: Early session had multiple full-stop losses hit quickly. Mid/late session execution improved materially: waited for confirmations more often, cut weak trades early, and avoided forcing in dead zones/chop. Key proactive risk action: cut a weakening setup early for tiny damage/profit instead of letting full SL hit.
entries.psychology: Main challenge was the urge to enter early for a better price and the urge to take profit too early. Big improvement: recognized and interrupted revenge-trade impulses. Strong anchor used repeatedly: “Missing is free, forcing is expensive.”
entries.ruleAdherence: Improved adherence as the session progressed. Confirmation/retest logic was followed more consistently later, but there were still early-session violations through lower-quality and less-confirmed entries.
entries.whatIDidGood: Avoided multiple low-quality entries by waiting for one more confirmation. Protected capital with early exits when reclaim thesis weakened. Reduced emotional overtrading in the second half. Stayed coachable and adjusted behavior in real time.
entries.whatToImprove: First 60–90 minutes: tighten filters to avoid large initial losses. Keep loss size tighter when setup does not confirm quickly. Maintain better payoff symmetry so average loss is not several times larger than average win. Continue avoiding dead-zone trades and chop participation.
entries.lessonLearned: High win rate alone does not fix poor payoff structure. The repaired trade log shows 21 trades, gross P&L $-110.00, avg win $14.50, avg loss $-44.71, and profit factor 0.65. The next level is smaller losers, not more accuracy.
entries.nextTradeRule: No confirmation, no trade. If reclaim setup does not hold quickly, cut early with no hope-holding. Keep repeating: Missing is free. Forcing is expensive. During chop-box conditions: stand down until break + retest hold.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Thursday, March 12, 2026
entries.mood: Overall solid and more controlled than yesterday. Session had a good rhythm and cleaner confidence. Green day, but still a little too willing to let a few losers get bigger than they should.
entries.content: Session summary: Green day with strong consistency and high win rate. The biggest positive was repeatable small-win execution. The biggest issue is still the same: a few losers remain too large compared with the average winner. Real progress day, but the next level comes from shrinking losses and giving winners more room.
entries.setupContext: Primary instrument: MNQ. Session favored quick scalp-style executions. Conditions allowed repeated small green trades, but still punished any lapse in discipline. The edge was in short-duration momentum pops, not in giving trades extra room.
entries.execution: Strong consistency on quick winners. Average hold time stayed short, around 1 min 23 sec. Best stretch showed clean in-and-out execution without forcing. Problem trades came from letting a few setups go too far against me instead of invalidating faster.
entries.psychology: Better overall emotional control than prior red days. No obvious revenge spiral in the numbers. Main mental leak remains the same: tolerance for oversized losers and a tendency to protect small winners too quickly. I can now clearly see the pattern: babysitting losers and suffocating winners.
entries.ruleAdherence: Good adherence on entries and quick profit-taking. Weak adherence on loss containment. Green day, but still not fully professional because the risk structure is off: losers are too large relative to the size of normal winners.
entries.whatIDidGood: Stayed green through consistency, not random luck. Stacked a large number of small winners. Maintained a strong win rate without an emotional blowup. Traded with better rhythm and more control than the previous session.
entries.whatToImprove: Cut losers faster when the trade does not work quickly. Stop allowing one bad trade to wipe out multiple clean wins. Improve payoff symmetry so the average loss is not several times larger than the average winner. Let good trades breathe more instead of taking safety too early.
entries.lessonLearned: I do not need more accuracy. I need tighter invalidation. The problem is not that I’m wrong too often — it’s that when I’m wrong, I stay wrong too long. On the other side, when I’m right, I often grab relief too early instead of letting the trade work. The clean mental model: don’t babysit losers and suffocate winners.
entries.nextTradeRule: If the trade is not working, cut it faster instead of hoping. If the trade is working, let it work until the rule says exit — not fear. No more waiting for the hot stove to cool down. Take the hand off. Good trades get time. Bad trades lose time.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Friday, March 13, 2026
entries.mood: Emotionally messy session. Started with too much re-engagement and never really got fully back under control. The day felt less like structured trading and more like getting pulled back into the arena repeatedly.
entries.content: Session summary: Red day driven by severe overtrading and breakdown of discipline. The edge was still there, but it was buried under too many trades. Biggest takeaway: this session was lost more through behavior than through market conditions.
entries.setupContext: Primary instrument: MNQ. There were real opportunities and some good trades on the board. The issue was not lack of setups. The issue was excessive re-entry, overexposure, and breakdown of session discipline.
entries.execution: There were meaningful winners throughout the day, including several larger wins. This proves the edge was present. The problem was not trade quality alone, but trade quantity. Too many repeated decisions degraded execution and turned the session into noise.
entries.psychology: This became a self-control day more than a skill day. The need to keep trading overpowered the need to protect the day. Once the session got extended too far, discipline degraded and the market stopped being the main problem.
entries.ruleAdherence: Poor overall. The biggest violation was the total number of trades. A day with a hard max of 3 trades turned into 55 trades. At that point, the session moved far outside the intended operating framework.
entries.whatIDidGood: Still showed the ability to capture real upside. Produced multiple meaningful winners, including a +103 trade. Proved that when execution is aligned, the edge is still there.
entries.whatToImprove: Stop after the planned trade limit. Eliminate repeated low-value re-engagements. Protect the session from turning into a marathon of emotional decision-making. Tighten loss control and reduce exposure after early damage.
entries.lessonLearned: This was not mainly a market problem. It was a discipline and overtrading problem. I do not need more setups. I need fewer decisions and stronger stop rules around session limits. Repaired stats: 55 trades, gross $-206.50, net after $1/trade commissions $-261.50, win rate 56.4%, avg win $22.58, avg loss $-39.41, profit factor 0.77.
entries.nextTradeRule: Maximum 3 trades per day means maximum 3 trades per day. After the limit is hit, platform closed, no exceptions. No more trying to trade out of prior mistakes. Protect the day before trying to improve the day.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Sunday, March 15, 2026
entries.content: Session summary: Logged MNQ trade from today. Small green outcome, but the main value was the lesson on waiting for reclaim + follow-through instead of entering on the first curl out of chop.
entries.setupContext: Watched 5m/1m BOS-based long structure on MNQ. The setup idea was valid directionally, but the first entry came before clean confirmation out of chop.
entries.execution: Entered long 24497.50 and exited 24505.00 for +$15. Trade was green, but entry quality was early. The cleaner confirmation would have been reclaim + follow-through above the nearby micro highs, not just curling upward from support.
entries.psychology: Good recovery. Stayed honest about the early entry instead of rationalizing it. Took the better exit and used the trade as feedback instead of forcing a bigger outcome.
entries.ruleAdherence: Mixed but improving. The entry was early relative to the full setup rule, but the exit discipline and post-trade review were solid.
entries.whatIDidGood: Recognized the setup logic was not random, accepted that confirmation was still incomplete, exited cleanly, and learned from the difference between a curl and a true reclaim.
entries.whatToImprove: Wait for real separation from chop. A small upward curl is not enough. Need reclaim plus follow-through before committing.
entries.lessonLearned: Confirmation = reclaim + follow-through, not just curling. Wait for price to prove it can lift out of the mess before entering.
entries.nextTradeRule: Do not enter on the first hint of curl from support. Wait for reclaim + follow-through above the nearby micro high, then enter.

entries.title: Trading Journal — Monday, March 16, 2026
entries.content: Session ended green at +$53.50 across 10 MNQ trades. Biggest deeper takeaway now includes a core mindset breakthrough from the night before: rules exist to protect both mental capital and account capital by containing emotional spirals when trades fail.
entries.setupContext: Several valid-looking setups appeared, but not all stayed healthy after entry. The recurring issue was staying too active and taking too many trades rather than waiting for the clearest A+ opportunities.
entries.execution: Took 10 trades on MNQ. Net result +$53.50 with 7 wins, 2 losses, and 1 flat. Biggest loss was -$62.50 early, followed by strong recovery trades. The session still drifted into lower-value activity after recovery.
entries.psychology: Important realization: the real danger is not just market risk but losing control after accepting a loss badly. Following rules helps pre-accept risk, prevents mental spiraling, and protects capital from self-inflicted damage.
entries.ruleAdherence: Mixed. Better exits on weakening setups, but overtrading remained the main violation. Rules now need to be viewed as emotional containment tools, not just trade filters.
entries.whatIDidGood: Recognized setup weakness faster, exited to protect capital, and articulated the deeper reason rules matter.
entries.whatToImprove: Trade less. Accept risk before entry. Use rules to prevent emotional spirals after losses.
entries.lessonLearned: I need to follow rules to protect my mind. Protecting my mind protects my capital. If risk is accepted up front, I do not need to spiral when a trade fails.
entries.nextTradeRule: Before entry: define risk, accept the loss mentally, and commit to following the rule set so I do not spiral if the market goes the wrong way.
